calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Constance Figuers for Henry Gonzalez, Co-Trustee.
Sandra Benabou for Humberto Gonzalez.

The Court calls the matter, stating the petition is seeking various relief, and inquires why funds couldn't be released on existing order. Ms. Benabou stated the trustee's fees are in a blocked account and they can't make distribution without a court order.
The Petition for Orders to 1) Approve Co-trustee's Account and Report; etc., is taken under submission, subject to reviewing order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Petition Against Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Trust And For Orders: 1) Compelling Co-Trustee To Account; 2) Surcharging Co-Trustee; 3) Denying Co-Truste
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Feb-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Against Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Trust And For Orders: 1) Compelling Co-Trustee To Account; 2) Surcharging Co-Trustee; 3) Denying Co-Truste
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Bass for and with Humberto Gonzalez.
Steven Cross for Henry Gonzalez.

The Court noted that this is a continued matter, that it does not have a file, that it does not have any notes and that it is not aware of what the issues are. A hearing was held at the insistence of Mr. Bass. Mr. Bass stated that there is an urgent matter, that ever since the house was sold Humberto has been sleeping on couches, that the Beneficiaries have agreed to a preliminary distribution, that he submitted this by way of an ex parte petition three weeks ago and that he has not heard anything. Mr. Bass also stated that he also filed an ex parte petition for attorney's fees, that they are coming to the end of administration and requested that there Petition for Breach of Trust be put out for 90-days. The Court stated that it will flag the ex parte petition, raised the issue of whether or not ex parte petitions are a proper form of filing for what they are requesting and stated that the Parties may have to file a fully noticed petition for preliminary distribution. The Petition Against Co-Trustee for Breaches of Trust, etc. is further continued from December 18, 2018 to February 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

Petition Against Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Trust And For Orders: 1) Compelling Co-Trustee To Account; 2) Surcharging Co-Trustee; 3) Denying Co-Truste
Off Calendar Aug-07-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
